cod: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cocama.
coir: noun. The fibre obtained from the husk of a coconut, used chiefly in making rope, matting and as a peat substitute.
coo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Comox.
cor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Cornish.
cord: noun. A long, thin, flexible length of twisted yarns (strands) of fibre (a rope, for example).
corridor: noun. A narrow hall or passage with rooms leading off it, as in a building or in a railway carriage.
doc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern Kam.
door: noun. A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, typically consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge.
dor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dori'o.
odic: adjective. Of or pertaining to odes.
odor: noun. Any smell, whether fragrant or offensive.
orc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orma.
ordo: noun. A musical phrase constructed from one or more statements of one modal pattern and ending in a rest.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
roc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cacgia Roglai.
rod: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rogo.
rood: noun. A crucifix, cross, especially in a church.
